MySQL plugin R41-4
This library uses the enhanced pawndoc.xsl from pawn-lang/pawndoc. This XSL has features such as library and markdown support, and will not render this message when used. This is the sscanf plugin, which provides the sscanf function to extract basic structured data from strings. This is slightly different to regular expressions, but both have their place. A regular expression gives you total control over the exact structure of data down to the character level; however, extracting structured data like numbers using it is tricky. Conversely this gives slightly higher-level specifiers which can easily extract data types, at the expense of fine-grained control. To convert a string in to two numbers would look like: new num1, num2;
sscanf("45 100", "ii", num1, num2); ii is the specifier string, which here means "integer integer"; stating that the input string should be two whole numbers in a row (which is - "45 100"). num1 and num2 are the destination variables to store the found numbers in (after conversion from strings). You can check if the conversion failed by looking for a non-naught return value: new num1, num2;
if (sscanf("hello 100", "ii", num1, num2))
{
printf("The input was not two numbers.");
} This will fail because "hello" is not a whole number (or indeed any type of number at all). For more information on using the function refer to the tutorials or the reference documentation in the attached readme.

member.name: C:SSCANF_VERSION
member.remarks:
The sscanf version in BCD as a proper constant. Example: 2.10.3
=> 02 10 03
=> 0x021003

member.name: C:__namemax
member.summary: The maximum length of symbol names, excluding any NULLs.

member.name: F:CELLMIN_ON_MATCHES
F:MATCH_NAME_FIRST
member.remarks:
The MATCH_NAME_FIRST option as a constant string so you can get compile- time spell checking on the name. Specify whether u etc keep searching for better matching player names after finding one: SSCANF_Option(MATCH_NAME_FIRST, 0); // Enable scanning for the best name match not the first (default).
SSCANF_Option(MATCH_NAME_FIRST, 1); // Enable scanning for the first name match not the best (old behaviour).

member.name: F:MATCH_NAME_PARTIAL
member.remarks: The MATCH_NAME_PARTIAL option as a constant string so you can get compile-time spell checking on the name. Currently sscanf will search for players by name, and will always search for player whose name starts with the specified string. If someone types Y_Less, sscanf will not find say [CLAN]Y_Less because there name doesn't start with the specified text. This option, when set to 1, will search anywhere in the player's name for the given string.

member.name: F:MATCH_NAME_SIMILARITY
member.remarks:
The MATCH_NAME_SIMILARITY option as a constant string so you can get compile-time spell checking on the name. Specify whether u etc should use the ngrams-based similarity algorithm when searching for players matching a given name. I.e. enable or disable fuzzy name matching. It will return the best match found, but depending on the threshold that may still be very different to what was typed. SSCANF_Option(MATCH_NAME_SIMILARITY, 0.0); // Will find all names, regardless of input.
SSCANF_Option(MATCH_NAME_SIMILARITY, 1.0); // Will find exact matches only.
SSCANF_Option(MATCH_NAME_SIMILARITY, 0.5); // Will find names somewhat similar to the input.
SSCANF_Option(MATCH_NAME_SIMILARITY, -1.0); // Disable fuzzy name matching.

member.name: F:OLD_DEFAULT_CUSTOM
member.remarks: The OLD_DEFAULT_CUSTOM option as a constant string so you can get compile-time spell checking on the name. This is the same as OLD_DEFAULT_KUSTOM, but with an alternate spelling.

member.name: F:OLD_DEFAULT_KUSTOM
member.remarks: The OLD_DEFAULT_KUSTOM option as a constant string so you can get compile-time spell checking on the name. As with U, K used to require a valid identifier as the default and would parse it using the specified callback, so this would not work: K<vehicle>(Veyron) Because that is not a valid vehicle name in GTA. The new version now just takes a number and returns that regardless: K<vehicle>(999) This setting reverts to the old behaviour.

member.name: F:OLD_DEFAULT_NAME
member.remarks: The OLD_DEFAULT_NAME option as a constant string so you can get compile-time spell checking on the name. The behaviour of U, Q and R have been changed to take any number as a default, instead of a connected player. Setting OLD_DEFAULT_NAME to 1 will revert to the old version.

member.name: F:SSCANF_ALPHA
member.remarks: The SSCANF_ALPHA option as a constant string so you can get compile- time spell checking on the name. Specify the default alpha value for colours (m) which don't manually specify an alpha channel. The alpha values are specified as a decimal number, not a hex number, so setting an alpha of 0x80 would be: SSCANF_Option(SSCANF_ALPHA, 128);

member.name: F:SSCANF_ARGB
member.remarks:
The SSCANF_ARGB option as a constant string so you can get compile- time spell checking on the name. Specify whether the returned colour is ARGB or RGBA: SSCANF_Option(SSCANF_ARGB, 1); // Set 3- and 6-digit colour outputs to `AARRGGBB`.
SSCANF_Option(SSCANF_ARGB, 0); // Set 3- and 6-digit colour outputs to `RRGGBBAA` (default).

member.name: F:SSCANF_COLOUR_FORMS
member.remarks:
The SSCANF_COLOUR_FORMS option as a constant string so you can get compile-time spell checking on the name. There are multiple valid colour input formats, which you can enable or disable here. The parameter is a bit map (flags) for all the following values:
1 - #RGB
2 - #RRGGBB
4 - 0xRRGGBB
8 - RRGGBB
16 - {RRGGBB}
32 - 0xRRGGBBAA
64 - RRGGBBAA So to ONLY accept SA:MP `SendClientMessage` colours use: SSCANF_Option(SSCANF_COLOUR_FORMS, 16); To only accept 8-digit values use: SSCANF_Option(SSCANF_COLOUR_FORMS, 96); Default values (those specified between ()s for M) ignore this setting - they can always use any form.

member.name: F:SSCANF_EXT_
member.remarks: The fallback for the filename in extract on the old compiler, which doesn't have the inbuilt __file macro. This is the "feature" enabled by SSCANF_NO_NICE_FEATURES. Appends "unknown file" in the plugin when line number < 0.

member.name: F:SSCANF_FOM_
member.remarks: The fallback for the filename in unformat on the old compiler, which doesn't have the inbuilt __file macro. This is the "feature" enabled by SSCANF_NO_NICE_FEATURES. Appends "unknown file" in the plugin when line number < 0.

member.name: F:SSCANF_NPC
member.remarks: Was sscanf built for an NPC mode or a normal mode?

member.name: F:SSCANF_QUIET
member.remarks: The SSCANF_QUIET option as a constant string so you can get compile- time spell checking on the name. Don't print any errors to the console. Really not recommended unless you know your code is stable and in production.

member.name: F:SSCANF_UNK_
member.remarks: The fallback for the filename in sscanf on the old compiler, which doesn't have the inbuilt __file macro. This is the "feature" enabled by SSCANF_NO_NICE_FEATURES. Appends "unknown file" in the plugin when line number < 0.

member.name: F:SSCANF_VERSION_BCD
member.remarks:
The sscanf version in BCD. Example: 2.10.3
=> 02 10 03
=> 0x021003

member.name: F:SSCANF_VERSION_BUILD
member.remarks: The sscanf build number.

member.name: F:SSCANF_VERSION_MAJOR
member.remarks: The sscanf major version number.

member.name: F:SSCANF_VERSION_MINOR
member.remarks: The sscanf minor version number.

member.name: F:SSCANF_VERSION_STRING
member.remarks: The sscanf version as a string. E.g. "2.8.1".

member.name: F:SSCANF_gInit
member.remarks: Has sscanf been initialised at least once already in this script? Avoids the need to define FILTERSCRIPT by including OnFilterScriptInit and OnGameModeInit (plus OnNPCModeInit) and seeing which one gets called first.

member.name: F:__OPEN_MP_VERSION
member.remarks: If running on SA:MP this will remain 0, open.mp will set it. open.mp versions look like: 01.04.02.0544 Thus they are encoded here as: (major << 28) | (minor << 21) | (patch << 14) | (prerel)

member.name: F:__SAMP_INCLUDES_VERSION
member.remarks:
Version examples:
0.3.DL R1 - 03D010
0.3.7 R3 - 037030
0.3.7 R2-2 - 037022
0.3.7 R1-2 - 037012
0.3.7 - 037000
0.3z R4 - 030700
0.3z R3 - 030700
0.3z R2-1 - 030700
0.3z R1-2 - 030700
0.3z - 030700
0.3x R2 patch 1 - 030621
0.3x R2 - 030620
0.3x R1-2 - 030612
0.3x - 030600
0.3e - 030500
0.3d - 030400
0.3c - 030300
0.3b - 030200
0.3a - 030100
0.2X - 02A000
0.2.2 R3 - 022300 Rough rules:
Uses (roughtly) BCD. Special versions are denoted outside 0-9. 0.1.2c R4-5 | | || | | 0 1 23 4 5 = 0x012345 (assuming c is the third revision)
0.2X becomes 02A000 because it is basically 0.2.3, but not, while higher than 0.2.2 so can't be 020400 (for example). Also, its a capital letter, so doesn't use the revision method.
P.S. Making a consistent scheme for SA:MP versions is REALLY hard.
open.mp releases can use `A` as the first digit.

member.name: M:@timer
member.summary: A timer event occurred.
member.remarks:
This function executes after the configured delay or interval expires. See settimer to set the delay or interval. Depending on the timing precision of the host, the call may occur later than the delay that was set.
If the timer was set as a "single-shot", it must be ex plicitly set again for a next execution for the @timerfunction. If the timer is set to be repetitive, @timer will continue to be called with the set interval until it is disabled with another call to settimer.
member.returns: The return value of this function is currently ignored.

member.name: M:AddBomba
member.syntax: AddBomba(playerid, type, vehicleSQLID, Xbom, Ybom, Zbom, objectid)

member.name: M:AddMenuItem
member.param: Menu The menu ID to add an item to
The column to add the item to
[] The text for the new menu item. May be optionally formatted.
member.summary: Adds an item to a specified menu.

ul.li: Crashes when passed an invalid menu ID.
You can only have 12 items per menu (13th goes to the right side of the header of column name (coloured), 14th and higher not display at all).
You can only use 2 columns (0 and 1).
You can only add 8 colour codes per one item (~r~, ~g~ etc.).
Maximum length of menu item is 31 symbols.

member.returns: The index of the row this item was added to.

member.name: M:AddStaticVehicle
member.syntax: AddStaticVehicle(modelid, spawnX, spawnY, spawnZ, angle, colour1, colour2)
member.param: The Model ID for the vehicle
Float The x coordinate of the spawnpoint of this vehicle
Float The y coordinate of the spawnpoint of this vehicle
Float The z coordinate of the spawnpoint of this vehicle
Float Direction of vehicle - angle
The primary colour ID. -1 for random (random colour chosen by client)
The secondary colour ID. -1 for random (random colour chosen by client)
member.summary: Adds a 'static' vehicle (models are pre-loaded for players) to the gamemode.

ul.li: The vehicle ID of the vehicle created (between 1 and MAX_VEHICLES).
INVALID_VEHICLE_ID (65535) if vehicle was not created (vehicle limit reached or invalid vehicle model ID passed).

member.remarks: Can only be used when the server first starts (under OnGameModeInit).

member.name: M:AddVCP
member.syntax: AddVCP(playerid, objectid, Xv, Yv, Zv, ZZv)

member.name: M:AddVehicleComponent
member.param: The ID of the vehicle to add the component to. Not to be confused with modelid
The ID of the component to add to the vehicle
member.summary: Adds a 'component' (often referred to as a 'mod' (modification)) to a vehicle. Valid components can be found here.

ul.li: 0 - The component was not added because the vehicle does not exist.
1 - The component was successfully added to the vehicle.

member.remarks: Using an invalid component ID crashes the player's game. There are no internal checks for this.

member.name: M:ApplyAnimation
member.syntax: ApplyAnimation(playerid, animationLibrary[], animationName[], delta, loop, lockX, lockY, freeze, time, forceSync)
member.param: The ID of the player to apply the animation to
[] The animation library from which to apply an animation
[] The name of the animation to apply, within the specified library
Float The speed to play the animation (use 4.1)
bool If set to 1, the animation will loop. If set to 0, the animation will play once
bool If set to 0, the player is returned to their old x coordinate once the animation is complete (for animations that move the player such as walking). 1 will not return them to their old position
bool Same as above but for the y axis. Should be kept the same as the previous parameter
bool Setting this to 1 will freeze the player at the end of the animation. 0 will not
Timer in milliseconds. For a never-ending loop it should be 0

t_FORCE_SYNC Set to 1 to make server sync the animation with all other players in streaming radius. 2 works same as 1, but will ONLY apply the animation to streamed-in players, but NOT the actual player being animated (useful for npc animations and persistent animations when players are being streamed) (optional=0)
member.summary: Apply an animation to a player.
member.remarks: The optional parameter, which defaults to 0, in most cases is not needed since players sync animations themselves. The parameter can force all players who can see to play the animation regardless of whether the player is performing that animation. This is useful in circumstances where the player can't sync the animation themselves. For example, they may be paused.
An invalid animation library will crash the player's game.
member.returns: This function always returns 1, even if the player specified does not exist, or any of the parameters are invalid (e.g. invalid library).

member.name: M:Attach3DTextLabelToPlayer
member.param: Text3D The ID of the 3D text label to attach. Returned by Create3DTextLabel
The ID of the player to attach the label to
Float The x offset from the player
Float The y offset from the player
Float The z offset from the player
member.summary: Attach a 3D text label to a player.
member.returns:
1: The function executed successfully.
0: The function failed to execute. This means the player and/or label do not exist.

member.name: M:AttachObjectToVehicle
member.syntax: AttachObjectToVehicle(objectid, parentid, offsetX, offsetY, offsetZ, rotationX, rotationY, rotationZ)
member.param: The ID of the object to attach to the vehicle. Note that this is an object ID, not a model ID. The object must be CreateObject created first
Float The x axis offset from the vehicle to attach the object to
Float The y axis offset from the vehicle to attach the object to
Float The z axis offset from the vehicle to attach the object to
Float The x rotation offset for the object
Float The y rotation offset for the object
Float The z rotation offset for the object
member.summary: Attach an object to a vehicle.
member.param: The ID of the vehicle to attach the object to
member.remarks: The object must be created first.

When the vehicle is destroyed or respawned, the attached objects won't be destroyed with it; they will remain stationary at the position the vehicle disappeared and be reattached to the next vehicle to claim the vehicle ID that the objects were attached to.

member.name: M:AttachTrailerToVehicle
member.param: The ID of the vehicle that will be pulled
The ID of the vehicle that will pull the trailer
member.summary: Attach a vehicle to another vehicle as a trailer.
member.remarks: This will only work if both vehicles are streamed in for a player (check IsVehicleStreamedIn).
member.returns: This function always returns 1, even if neither of the vehicle IDs passed are valid.

member.name: M:BCD
member.remarks: Converts a version string to BCD. For example: BCD(5.6.17); Returns: 0x050617 Each section (between dots) is assigned a single byte and the last section is always in the lowest byte. This implies a maximum of four sections and two digits per section.

member.name: M:BanEx
member.param: The ID of the player to ban
[] The reason for the ban
member.summary: Ban a player with a reason.
member.remarks: Any action taken directly before Ban() (such as sending a message with SendClientMessage) will not reach the player. A timer must be used to delay the ban.

member.name: M:CallLocalFunction
member.param: [] Public function's name
[] Tag/format of each variable
{Bit,Bitmap,XML,XMLEntry,Group,INI,Language,Style,DB,DBResult,Menu,Text,PlayerText,Text3D,PlayerText3D,File,Float,CUSTOM_TAG_TYPES,_} 'Indefinite' number of arguments of any tag
member.summary: Calls a public function from the script in which it is used.
member.returns: The value that the only public function returned.
member.remarks: CallLocalFunction crashes the server if it's passing an empty string.

Specifier string placeholders:
c - a single character
d - an integer (whole) number
i - an integer (whole) number
x - a number in hexadecimal notation
f - a floating point number
s - a string

member.name: M:CancelEdit
member.param: The ID of the player to cancel edition for
member.summary: Cancel object edition mode for a player.

member.name: M:ChangeVehicleColours
member.param: The ID of the vehicle to change the colours of
The new vehicle's primary colour ID
The new vehicle's secondary colour ID
member.summary: Change a vehicle's primary and secondary colours.
member.remarks: Some vehicles have only a primary colour and some can not have the colour changed at all. A few (cement, squallo) have 4 colours, of which 2 can not currently be changed.
member.returns:
1: The function executed successfully. The vehicle's colour was successfully changed.
0: The function failed to execute. The vehicle does not exist.

member.name: M:ChangeVehiclePaintjob
member.param: The ID of the vehicle to change the paintjob of
The ID of the Paintjob to apply. Use 3 to remove a paintjob
member.summary: Change a vehicle's paintjob (for plain colours see ChangeVehicleColor).
member.remarks:
Known Bugs:
This function calls OnVehicleRespray.
Vehicles change their colour to white anymore when a paintjob is removed.
member.returns: This function always returns 1 (success), even if the vehicle passed is not created.

member.name: M:ClearAnimations
member.param: The ID of the player to clear the animations of
t_FORCE_SYNC Set to 1 to force playerid to sync the animation with other players in streaming radius (optional=0)
member.summary: Clears all animations for the given player (it also cancels all current tasks such as jetpacking,parachuting,entering vehicles, driving (removes player out of vehicle), swimming, etc.. ).
member.remarks: ClearAnimations doesn't do anything when the animation ends if we pass 1 for the freeze parameter in ApplyAnimation.

Unlike some other ways to remove player from a vehicle, this will also reset the vehicle's velocity to zero, instantly stopping the car. Player will appear on top of the vehicle with the same location as he was in his car seat.
member.returns: This function always returns 1, even when the player specified is not connected.

The virtual world in which you are able to see the 3D Text
bool Test the line-of-sight so this text can't be seen through objects (optional=0)

member.name: M:CreateMenu
member.syntax: CreateMenu(title[], columns, x, y, column1width, column2width, ...)
member.param: [] The title for the new menu. May be optionally formatted.
How many colums shall the new menu have
Float The x position of the menu (640x460 canvas - 0 would put the menu at the far left)
Float The y position of the menu (640x460 canvas - 0 would put the menu at the far top)
Float The width for the first column
Float The width for the second column (optional=0.0)
member.summary: Creates a menu.
member.remarks:
This function merely CREATES the menu - ShowMenuForPlayer must be used to show it.
You can only create and access 2 columns (0 and 1).
If the title's length is equal to or greater than 32 chars the title is truncated to 30 characters.
There is a limit of 12 items per menu, and a limit of 128 menus in total.
member.returns: The ID of the new menu or -1 on failure.

member.name: M:CreateObject
member.syntax: CreateObject(modelid, x, y, z, rotationX, rotationY, rotationZ, drawDistance)
member.param: The model to create
Float The x coordinate to create the object at
Float The y coordinate to create the object at
Float The z coordinate to create the object at
Float The x rotation of the object
Float The y rotation of the object
Float The z rotation of the object
Float The distance that San Andreas renders objects at. 0.0 will cause objects to render at their default distances. Usable since 0.3b, limited to 300 prior to 0.3x (optional=0.0)
member.summary: Creates an object at specified coordinates in the game world.
member.remarks: Objects that emit light (lampposts, police lights, bollard lights, neons etc.) that have a greater rotation than 16.26 degrees (or -16.26) on either the x or y axis will stop shining. This effect also applies to light objects attached to other objects, players and vehicles. If a light object is attached to a car and the car is rotated over 16.26 degrees (like in a rollover), the object will also stop emitting light. This is a GTA:SA issue and is not caused by a bug in open.mp.

In case the light is attached to another object, one fix for this is to set SyncRotation to false in AttachObjectToObject. This will ensure the light stays at 0 rotation. This would only really work for objects that consist ONLY of light, so wouldn't work for the police light for example.
There is a limit of 1000 objects (MAX_OBJECTS). To circumvent this limit, you can use a streamer
member.returns: The ID of the object that was created, or INVALID_OBJECT_ID if the object limit (MAX_OBJECTS) was reached.

member.name: M:CreatePlayerTextDraw
member.param: The ID of the player to create the textdraw for
Float x-Coordinate
Float y-Coordinate
[] The text in the textdraw. May be optionally formatted.
member.summary: Creates a textdraw for a single player. This can be used as a way around the global text-draw limit.
member.remarks:
If you choose values for y that are less than 1, the first text row will be invisible and only the shadow is visible.
If part of the text is off-screen, the colour of the text will not show, only the shadow (if enabled) will.

Maximum length of textdraw is 800 characters.
If you use colour codes (such as ~R~ ~G~) beyond 255th character the legacy (SA:MP) client will crash trying to display the textdraw.
Keyboard key mapping codes (such as ~k~~VEHICLE_ENTER_EXIT~ Doesn't work beyond 255th character.

The x, y coordinate is the top left coordinate for the text draw area based on a 640x480 "canvas" (irrespective of screen resolution). If you plan on using PlayerTextDrawAlignment with alignment 3 (right), the x, y coordinate is the top right coordinate for the text draw.
This function merely CREATES the textdraw, you must use PlayerTextDrawShow to show it to a player.
It is recommended to use WHOLE numbers instead of decimal positions when creating player textdraws to ensure resolution friendly design.
Player-textdraws are automatically destroyed when a player disconnects.
member.returns: The ID of the created textdraw.

member.name: M:CreateTeleDynamicPickup
member.syntax: CreateTeleDynamicPickup(modelid, teleid, x, y, z, worldid, interiorid)

member.name: M:CreateVehicle

member.param: The model for the vehicle
Float The x coordinate for the vehicle

Float The z coordinate for the vehicle
Float The facing angle for the vehicle
The primary colour ID
The secondary colour ID
The delay until the car is respawned without a driver in seconds. Using -1 will prevent the vehicle from respawning
bool Added in 0.3.7; will not work in earlier versions. Enables the vehicle to have a siren, providing the vehicle has a horn (optional=false)
member.summary: Creates a vehicle in the world. Can be used in place of AddStaticVehicleEx at any time in the script.
member.remarks: Trains can only be added with AddStaticVehicle and AddStaticVehicleEx.
member.returns:
The vehicle ID of the vehicle created (1 to MAX_VEHICLES).
INVALID_VEHICLE_ID (65535) if vehicle was not created (vehicle limit reached or invalid vehicle model ID passed).

member.name: M:DetachTrailerFromVehicle

member.summary: Detach the connection between a vehicle and its trailer, if any.

member.name: M:DisableInteriorEnterExits
member.summary: Disable all the interior entrances and exits in the game (the yellow arrows at doors).
member.remarks: If the gamemode is changed after this function has been used, and the new gamemode doesn't disable markers, the markers will NOT reappear for already-connected players (but will for newly connected players).
This function will only work if it has been used BEFORE a player connects (it is recommended to use it in OnGameModeInit). It will not remove a connected player's markers.
member.returns: This function always returns 1.

member.name: M:DisablePlayerCheckpoint
member.param: The ID of the player whose checkpoint to disable
member.summary: Disables (hides/destroys) a player's set checkpoint. Players can only have a single checkpoint set at a time. Checkpoints don't need to be disabled before setting another one.
member.returns:
1: The function executed successfully. Success is also returned if the player doesn't have a checkpoint shown already.
0: The function failed to execute. This means the player is not connected.

member.name: M:DisablePlayerRaceCheckpoint
member.param: The player to disable the current checkpoint for
member.summary: Disable any initialized race checkpoints for a specific player, since you can only have one at any given time.

member.name: M:EnableStuntBonusForAll
member.param: bool 1 to enable stunt bonuses or 0 to disable them
member.summary: Enables or disables stunt bonuses for all players. If enabled, players will receive monetary rewards when performing a stunt in a vehicle (e.g. a wheelie).

member.name: M:EnableTirePopping
member.param: bool 1 to enable, 0 to disable tire popping
member.remarks: Tire popping is enabled by default. If you want to disable tire popping, you'll have to manually script it using OnVehicleDamageStatusUpdate.

member.name: M:GameTextForAll

The duration of the text being shown in milliseconds
The style of text to be displayed

The style of text to be displayed

member.name: M:GetActorPoolSize

member.name: M:GetMaxPlayers
member.summary: Returns the maximum number of players that can join the server, as set by the server variable 'maxplayers' in server.cfg.
member.remarks: This function can not be used in place of MAX_PLAYERS. It can not be used at compile time (e.g. for array sizes). MAX_PLAYERS should always be re-defined to what the 'maxplayers' var will be, or higher.
member.returns: The maximum number of players that can join the server.

member.name: M:GetPVarFloat
member.param: The ID of the player whose player variable you want to get
[] The name of the player variable
member.summary: Gets a player variable as a float.
member.returns: The float from the specified player variable.

member.name: M:GetPlayerAmmo
member.param: The ID of the player whose ammo to get
member.summary: Gets the amount of ammo in a player's current weapon.
member.remarks: The ammo can hold 16-bit values, therefore values over 32767 will return erroneous values.
member.returns: The amount of ammo in the player's current weapon.

member.name: M:GetPlayerArmour
member.param: The ID of the player that you want to get the armour of
Float & The float to to store the armour in, passed by reference
member.summary: This function stores the armour of a player into a variable.
member.remarks: Even though the armour can be set to near infinite values on the server side, the individual clients will only report values up to 255. Anything higher will wrap around; 256 becomes 0, 257 becomes 1, etc.
Armour is obtained rounded to integers: set 50.15, but get 50.0
The function's name is armour, not armor (Americanized).
member.returns:
1 - success.
0 - failure (i.e. player not connected).

member.name: M:GetPlayerDrunkLevel
member.param: The player you want to check the drunkenness level of
member.summary: Checks the player's level of drunkenness. If the level is less than 2000, the player is sober. The player's level of drunkness goes down slowly automatically (1 level per frame) but will always reach 2000 at the end (in 0.3b it will stop at 0). The higher drunkenness levels affect the player's camera, and the car driving handling. The level of drunkenness increases when the player drinks from a bottle (You can use SetPlayerSpecialAction to give them bottles).
member.returns: An integer with the level of drunkenness of the player.

member.name: M:GetPlayerFacingAngle
member.param: The player you want to get the angle of
Float & The Float to store the angle in, passed by reference
member.summary: Gets the angle a player is facing.
member.remarks: Angles are reversed in GTA:SA; 90 degrees would be East in the real world, but in GTA:SA 90 degrees is in fact West. North and South are still 0/360 and 180. To convert this, simply do 360 - angle.
Angles returned when inside a vehicle is rarely correct. To get the correct facing angle while inside a vehicle, use GetVehicleZAngle.

member.name: M:GetPlayerHealth
member.param: The ID of the player
Float & Float to store health, passed by reference
member.summary: The function GetPlayerHealth allows you to retrieve the health of a player. Useful for cheat detection, among other things.
member.remarks:
Even though the health can be set to near infinite values on the server side, the individual clients will only report values up to 255. Anything higher will wrap around; 256 becomes 0, 257 becomes 1, etc.
Health is obtained rounded to integers: set 50.15, but get 50.0
member.returns:
1 - success.
0 - failure (i.e. player not connected).

member.name: M:GetPlayerInterior
member.param: The player to get the interior ID of
member.summary: Retrieves the player's current interior. A list of currently known interiors with their positions can be found on this page.
member.remarks: Always returns 0 for NPCs.
member.returns: The interior ID the player is currently in.

member.name: M:GetPlayerIp
member.param: The ID of the player to get the IP address of
[] An array into which to store the player's IP address, passed by reference
The maximum length of the IP address (recommended 16)
member.summary: Get the specified player's IP address and store it in a string.
member.remarks: This function does not work when used in OnPlayerDisconnect because the player is already disconnected. It will return an invalid IP (255.255.255.255). Save players' IPs under OnPlayerConnect if they need to be used under OnPlayerDisconnect.
member.returns: 1 on success and 0 on failure.

member.name: M:GetPlayerKeys
member.param: The ID of the player to get the keys of
t_KEY & Bitmask containing the player's key states. List of keys
& Up/down state
& Left/right state
member.summary: Check which keys a player is pressing.
member.remarks: Only the FUNCTION of keys can be detected; not actual keys. For example, it is not possible to detect if a player presses SPACE, but you can detect if they press SPRINT (which can be mapped (assigned/binded) to ANY key (but is space by default)).
As of update 0.3.7, the keys "A" and "D" are not recognized when in a vehicle. However, keys "W" and "S" can be detected with the "keys" parameter.
member.returns: The keys are stored in the specified variables.

member.name: M:GetPlayerMenu
member.param: The ID of the player to get the current menu of
member.summary: Gets the ID of the menu the player is currently viewing (shown by ShowMenuForPlayer).
member.remarks: Returns previous menu when none is displayed.
member.returns: The ID of the player's currently shown menu, or INVALID_MENU (255) if no menu shown.

member.name: M:GetPlayerMoney

member.summary: Retrieves the amount of money a player has.

member.name: M:GetPlayerName
member.param: The ID of the player to get the name of
[] An array into which to store the name, passed by reference
The length of the string that should be stored. Recommended to be MAX_PLAYER_NAME
member.summary: Get a player's name.
member.remarks: A player's name can be up to 24 characters long (as of 0.3d R2) by using SetPlayerName. This is defined as 24 by default. However, the client can only join with a nickname between 3 and 20 characters, otherwise the connection will be rejected and the player has to quit to choose a valid name.
member.returns: The length of the player's name. 0 if player specified doesn't exist.

Float & A float variable in which to store the y coordinate in, passed by reference
Float & A float variable in which to store the z coordinate in, passed by reference

member.name: M:GetPlayerSkin
member.param: The player you want to get the skin from
member.summary: Returns the class of the players skin.
member.remarks: Returns the new skin after SetSpawnInfo is called but before the player actually respawns to get the new skin.
Returns the old skin if the player was spawned through SpawnPlayer function.
member.returns: The skin ID (0 if invalid).

member.name: M:GetPlayerSpecialAction
member.param: The ID of the player to get the special action of
member.summary: Retrieves a player's current special action.

remarks.b: Special actions: (marked with * cannot be set)

ul.li: 1 - SPECIAL_ACTION_DUCK * - Detect if the player is crouching.
3 - SPECIAL_ACTION_ENTER_VEHICLE * - Detect if the player is entering a vehicle via an animation.
4 - SPECIAL_ACTION_EXIT_VEHICLE * - Detect if the player is exiting a vehicle via an animation.
20 - SPECIAL_ACTION_DRINK_BEER - Will increase the player's drunk level when used
21 - SPECIAL_ACTION_SMOKE_CIGGY - Will give the player a cigar
22 - SPECIAL_ACTION_DRINK_WINE - Will give the player a wine bottle to get drunk from
23 - SPECIAL_ACTION_DRINK_SPRUNK - Will give the player a sprunk bottle to drink from
24 - SPECIAL_ACTION_CUFFED - Will force the player in to cuffs (hands are behind their back) (does not work on CJ skin)
25 - SPECIAL_ACTION_CARRY - Will apply a 'carrying' animation to the player and make them unable to sprint, jump or punch (does not work on CJ skin)
68 - SPECIAL_ACTION_PISSING - Will make make the player perform the pissing animation with visible pee.

member.returns: The special action of the player.

member.name: M:GetPlayerState
member.param: The ID of the player to get the current state of
member.summary: Get a player's current state.

ul.li: PLAYER_STATE_DRIVER - player is the driver of a vehicle
PLAYER_STATE_PASSENGER - player is passenger of a vehicle
PLAYER_STATE_WASTED - player is dead or on class selection
PLAYER_STATE_ENTER_VEHICLE_DRIVER - player enters a vehicle as driver
PLAYER_STATE_ENTER_VEHICLE_PASSENGER - player enters a vehicle as passenger

member.returns: The player's current state as an integer.

member.name: M:GetPlayerVehicleSeat

member.name: M:GetTickCount

member.remarks: GetTickCount will cause problems on servers with uptime of over 24 days as GetTickCount will eventually warp past the integer size constraints. However using this function fixes the problem.
One common use for GetTickCount is for benchmarking. It can be used to calculate how much time some code takes to execute.
member.returns: Uptime of the actual server (not the open.mp server).

member.name: M:Kick
member.param: The ID of the player to kick
member.summary: Kicks a player from the server. They will have to quit the game and re-connect if they wish to continue playing.
member.remarks: Any action taken directly before Kick() (such as sending a message with SendClientMessage) will not reach the player. A timer must be used to delay the kick.
member.returns: This function always returns 1, even if the function failed to execute (player specified doesn't exist).

member.name: M:ManualVehicleEngineAndLights
member.summary: Use this function before any player connects (OnGameModeInit) to tell all clients that the script will control vehicle engines and lights. This prevents the game automatically turning the engine on/off when players enter/exit vehicles and headlights automatically coming on when it is dark.
member.remarks: Is it not possible to reverse this function after it has been used. You must either use it or not use it.
member.returns: This function always returns 1. It cannot fail to execute.

members.member:
The ID of the player that responded to the dialog
The ID of the dialog the player responded to, assigned in ShowPlayerDialog
1 for left button and 0 for right button (if only one button shown, always 1)
The ID of the list item selected by the player (starts at 0) (only if using a list style dialog)
[] The text entered into the input box by the player or the selected list item text
omp_dialog This callback is called when a player responds to a dialog shown using ShowPlayerDialog by either clicking a button, pressing ENTER/ESC or double-clicking a list item (if using a list style dialog). A player's dialog doesn't hide when the gamemode restarts, causing the server to print "Warning: PlayerDialogResponse PlayerId: 0 dialog ID doesn't match last sent dialog ID" if a player responded to this dialog after restart. Parameters can contain different values, based on dialog's style. Returning 0 in this callback will pass the dialog to another script in case no matching code were found in your gamemode's callback.
It is always called first in filterscripts so returning 1 there blocks other filterscripts from seeing it.
Aqui :D

member.name: M:OnEnterExitModShop
member.param: The ID of the player that entered or exited the modshop
1 if the player entered or 0 if they exited
The interior ID of the modshop that the player is entering (or 0 if exiting)
member.summary: This callback is called when a player enters or exits a mod shop.
member.remarks: Players collide when they get into the same mod shop.
member.returns:
This callback does not handle returns.
It is always called first in filterscripts.

member.name: M:OnFilterScriptExit
member.summary: This callback is called when a filterscript is unloaded. It is only called inside the filterscript which is unloaded.
member.returns: This callback does not handle returns.

member.name: M:OnFilterScriptInit
member.summary: This callback is called when a filterscript is initialized (loaded). It is only called inside the filterscript which is starting.
member.returns: This callback does not handle returns.
member.remarks: Called when the script starts if it is a filterscript, sets up the system, then calls the "real" OnFilterScriptInit (using the new ALS 2 hook method).

member.name: M:OnGameModeExit
member.summary: This callback is called when a gamemode ends, either through 'gmx', the server being shut down, or GameModeExit.
member.remarks: This function can also be used in a filterscript to detect if the gamemode changes with RCON commands like changemode or gmx, as changing the gamemode does not reload a filterscript.

When using OnGameModeExit in conjunction with the 'rcon gmx' console command keep in mind there is a potential for client bugs to occur an example of this is excessive RemoveBuildingForPlayer calls during OnGameModeInit which could result in a client crash.
This callback will NOT be called if the server crashes or the process is killed by other means, such as using the Linux kill command or pressing the close-button on the Windows console.
member.returns:
0 - Will prevent other filterscripts from receiving this callback.
1 - Indicates that this callback will be passed to the next filterscript.
It is always called first in gamemode.

member.name: M:OnGameModeInit
member.summary: This callback is triggered when the gamemode starts.
member.remarks: This function can also be used in a filterscript to detect if the gamemode changes with RCON commands like changemode or gmx, as changing the gamemode does not reload a filterscript.
member.returns:
0 - Will prevent other filterscripts from receiving this callback.
1 - Indicates that this callback will be passed to the next filterscript.
It is always called first in gamemode.
member.remarks: Called when the script starts if it is a gamemode. This callback is also called in filterscripts so we don't want to reinitialise the system in that case.

member.name: M:OnPlayerClickPlayerTextDraw
member.param: The ID of the player that selected a textdraw
PlayerText The ID of the player-textdraw that the player selected
member.summary: This callback is called when a player clicks on a player-textdraw. It is not called when player cancels the select mode (ESC) - however, OnPlayerClickTextDraw is.
member.remarks: When a player presses ESC to cancel selecting a textdraw, OnPlayerClickTextDraw is called with a textdraw ID of INVALID_TEXT_DRAW. OnPlayerClickPlayerTextDraw won't be called also.
member.returns:
Returning 1 in this callback will prevent it being called in other scripts. This should be used to signal that the textdraw on which they clicked was 'found' and no further processing is needed. You should return 0 if the textdraw on which they clicked wasn't found, just like in OnPlayerCommandText.
It is always called first in filterscripts so returning 1 there also blocks other scripts from seeing it.

member.name: M:OnPlayerClickTextDraw
member.param: The ID of the player that clicked on the textdraw
Text The ID of the clicked textdraw. INVALID_TEXT_DRAW if selection was cancelled
member.summary: This callback is called when a player clicks on a textdraw or cancels the select mode with the Escape key.
member.remarks: The clickable area is defined by TextDrawTextSize. The x and y parameters passed to that function must not be zero or negative.
Do not use CancelSelectTextDraw unconditionally within this callback. This results in an infinite loop.
member.returns:
Returning 1 in this callback will prevent it being called in other scripts. This should be used to signal that the textdraw on which they clicked was 'found' and no further processing is needed. You should return 0 if the textdraw on which they clicked wasn't found, just like in OnPlayerCommandText.
It is always called first in filterscripts so returning 1 there also blocks other scripts from seeing it.

member.name: M:OnPlayerCommandText
member.param: The ID of the player that entered a command
[] The command that was entered (including the forward slash)
member.summary: This callback is called when a player enters a command into the client chat window. Commands are anything that start with a forward slash, e.g. /help.
member.remarks: This callback can also be called by NPC.
member.returns:
Return 1 if the command was processed, otherwise 0; If the command was not found both in filterscripts and in gamemode, the player will be received a message: SERVER: Unknown command.
It is always called first in filterscripts so returning 1 there blocks other scripts from seeing it.

member.name: M:OnPlayerConnect
member.param: The ID of the player that connected
member.summary: This callback is called when a player connects to the server.
member.remarks: This callback can also be called by NPC.
member.returns:
0 - Will prevent other filterscripts from receiving this callback.
1 - Indicates that this callback will be passed to the next filterscript.
It is always called first in filterscripts.
member.remarks: Called when a player connects. Actually increments an internal count so that if a script ends and OnPlayerDisconnect is called then sscanf still knows that the player is really connected. Also stores their name internally.

member.name: M:OnPlayerDeath
member.param: The ID of the player that died
The ID of the player that killed the player who died, or INVALID_PLAYER_ID if there was none
t_WEAPON The ID of the reason for the player's death
member.summary: This callback is called when a player dies, either by suicide or by being killed by another player.
member.remarks:
The reason will return 37 (flame thrower) from any fire sources (e.g. molotov, 18)
The reason will return 51 from any weapon that creates an explosion (e.g. RPG, grenade)
You do not need to check whether killerid is valid before using it in SendDeathMessage. INVALID_PLAYER_ID is a valid killerid ID parameter in that function.
playerid is the only one who can call the callback. (good to know for anti fake death)
member.returns:
0 - Will prevent other filterscripts from receiving this callback.
1 - Indicates that this callback will be passed to the next filterscript.
It is always called first in filterscripts.

member.name: M:OnPlayerDisconnect
member.param: The ID of the player that disconnected
The reason for the disconnection. See table below
member.summary: This callback is called when a player disconnects from the server.
member.remarks: This callback can also be called by NPC.

Some functions might not work correctly when used in this callback because the player is already disconnected when the callback is called. This means that you can't get unambiguous information from functions like GetPlayerIp and GetPlayerPos.

ul.li: 0 - timeout/Crash - the player's connection was lost. Either their game crashed or their network had a fault.
1 - quit - the player purposefully quit, either using the /quit (/q) command or via the pause menu.
2 - kick/ban - the player was kicked or banned by the server.

member.returns:
0 - Will prevent other filterscripts from receiving this callback.
1 - Indicates that this callback will be passed to the next filterscript.
It is always called first in filterscripts.
member.remarks: Called when a player disconnects, or when a script is ended.

member.name: M:OnPlayerFinishedDownloading
member.param: The ID of the player that finished downloading custom models
The ID of the virtual world the player finished downloading custom models for
member.summary: This callback is called when a player finishes downloading custom models. For more information on how to add custom models to your server, see the release thread and this tutorial.

member.name: M:OnPlayerGiveDamage
member.param: The ID of the player that gave damage
The ID of the player that received damage
Float The amount of health/armour damagedid has lost (combined)
t_WEAPON The reason that caused the damage
The body part that was hit.
member.summary: This callback is called when a player gives damage to another player.
member.remarks:
Keep in mind this function can be inaccurate in some cases.
If you want to prevent certain players from damaging eachother, use SetPlayerTeam.
The weaponid will return 37 (flame thrower) from any fire sources (e.g. molotov, 18)
The weaponid will return 51 from any weapon that creates an explosion (e.g. RPG, grenade)
playerid is the only one who can call the callback.
The amount is always the maximum damage the weaponid can do, even when the health left is less than that maximum damage. So when a player has 100.0 health and gets shot with a Desert Eagle which has a damage value of 46.2, it takes 3 shots to kill that player. All 3 shots will show an amount of 46.2, even though when the last shot hits, the player only has 7.6 health left.
member.returns:
1 - Callback will not be called in other filterscripts.
0 - Allows this callback to be called in other filterscripts.
It is always called first in filterscripts so returning 1 there blocks other filterscripts from seeing it.

member.name: M:OnPlayerGiveDamageActor
member.param: The ID of the player that gave damage
The ID of the actor that received damage
Float The amount of health/armour damaged_actorid has lost
t_WEAPON The reason that caused the damage
The body part that was hit
member.summary: This callback is called when a player gives damage to an actor.
member.remarks: This function does not get called if the actor is set invulnerable (WHICH IS BY DEFAULT). See SetActorInvulnerable.
member.returns:
1 - Callback will not be called in other filterscripts.
0 - Allows this callback to be called in other filterscripts.
It is always called first in filterscripts so returning 1 there blocks other filterscripts from seeing it.

member.name: M:OnPlayerTakeDamage
member.param: The ID of the player that took damage
The ID of the player that caused the damage. INVALID_PLAYER_ID if self-inflicted
Float The amount of damage the player took (health and armour combined)
t_WEAPON The ID of the weapon/reason for the damage
The body part that was hit.
member.summary: This callback is called when a player takes damage.
member.remarks: GetPlayerHealth and GetPlayerArmour will return the old amounts of the player before this callback.

The weaponid will return 37 (flame thrower) from any fire sources (e.g. molotov, 18).
The weaponid will return 51 from any weapon that creates an explosion (e.g. RPG, grenade)
playerid is the only one who can call the callback.
The amount is always the maximum damage the weaponid can do, even when the health left is less than that maximum damage. So when a player has 100.0 health and gets shot with a Desert Eagle which has a damage value of 46.2, it takes 3 shots to kill that player. All 3 shots will show an amount of 46.2, even though when the last shot hits, the player only has 7.6 health left.
member.returns:
1 - Callback will not be called in other filterscripts.
0 - Allows this callback to be called in other filterscripts.
It is always called first in filterscripts so returning 1 there blocks other filterscripts from seeing it.

member.name: M:OnPlayerText
member.param: The ID of the player who typed the text
[] The text the player typed
member.summary: Called when a player sends a chat message.
member.remarks: This callback can also be called by NPC.
member.returns:
Returning 0 in this callback will stop the text from being sent to all players.
It is always called first in filterscripts so returning 0 there blocks other scripts from seeing it.

member.name: M:OnPlayerUpdate
member.param: ID of the player sending an update packet
member.summary: This callback is called every time a client/player updates the server with their status. It is often used to create custom callbacks for client updates that aren't actively tracked by the server, such as health or armor updates or players switching weapons.
member.remarks: This callback can also be called by NPC.
This callback is called, on average, 30 times per second, per player; only use it when you know what it's meant for (or more importantly what it's NOT meant for).
The frequency with which this callback is called for each player varies, depending on what the player is doing. Driving or shooting will trigger a lot more updates than idling.
member.returns:
0 - Update from this player will not be replicated to other clients.
1 - Indicates that this update can be processed normally and sent to other players.
It is always called first in filterscripts.

member.name: M:OnPlayerWeaponShot
member.syntax: OnPlayerWeaponShot(playerid, weaponid, hittype, hitid, fX, fY, fZ)
member.param: The ID of the player that shot a weapon
t_WEAPON The ID of the weapon shot by the player
t_BULLET_HIT_TYPE The type of thing the shot hit (none, player, vehicle, or (player)object)
The ID of the player, vehicle or object that was hit
Float The X coordinate that the shot hit
Float The Y coordinate that the shot hit
Float The Z coordinate that the shot hit
member.summary: This callback is called when a player fires a shot from a weapon. Only bullet weapons are supported. Only passenger drive-by is supported (not driver drive-by, and not sea sparrow / hunter shots).
member.remarks: BULLET_HIT_TYPE_PLAYER is also called for NPCs. Actors are ignored by this callback and detects as BULLET_HIT_TYPE_NONE.
This callback is only called when lag compensation is enabled.

Isn't called if you fired in vehicle as driver or if you are looking behind with the aim enabled (shooting in air).
It is called as BULLET_HIT_TYPE_VEHICLE with the correct hitid (the hit player's vehicleid) if you are shooting a player which is in a vehicle. It won't be called as BULLET_HIT_TYPE_PLAYER at all.
GetPlayerLastShotVectors can be used in this callback for more detailed bullet vector information.
member.returns:
0 - Prevent the bullet from causing damage.
1 - Allow the bullet to cause damage.
It is always called first in filterscripts so returning 0 there also blocks other scripts from seeing it.

member.name: M:OnRconCommand
member.param: [] A string containing the command that was typed, as well as any passed parameters
member.summary: This callback is called when a command is sent through the server console, remote RCON, or via the in-game "/rcon command".
member.remarks: You will need to include this callback in a loaded filterscript for it to work in the gamemode!
"/rcon" is not included in "cmd" when a player types a command.
If you use the print function here, it will send a message to the player who typed the command in-game as well as the log.
This callback is not called when the player is not logged in as RCON admin.

When the player is not logged in as RCON admin and uses /rcon login, this callback will not be called and OnRconLoginAttempt is called instead. However, when the player is logged in as RCON admin, the use of this command will call this callback.
member.returns:
0 if the command was not processed, it will be passed to another script or 1 if the command was processed, will not be passed to other scripts.
It is always called first in filterscripts so returning 1 there blocks gamemode from seeing it.

member.name: M:OnRconLoginAttempt
member.param: [] The IP of the player that tried to log in to RCON
[] The password used to login with
0 if the password was incorrect or 1 if it was correct
member.summary: This callback is called when someone attempts to log in to RCON in-game; successful or not.
member.remarks: This callback is only called when /rcon login is used in-game.
This callback is only called when the player is not yet logged in. When the player is logged in, OnRconCommand is called instead.
member.returns:
This callback does not handle returns.
It is always called first in filterscripts.

member.name: M:OnScriptUnloadPlayer

member.name: M:OnTrailerUpdate
member.param: The ID of the player who sent a trailer update
The Trailer being updated
member.summary: This callback is called when a player sent a trailer update.
member.remarks: This callback is called very frequently per second per trailer. You should refrain from implementing intensive calculations or intensive file writing/reading operations in this callback.
member.returns:
0 - Cancels any trailer updates from being sent to other players. Update is still sent to the updating player.
1 - Processes the trailer update as normal and synchronizes it between all players.
It is always called first in filterscripts.

member.name: M:OnUnoccupiedVehicleUpdate
member.syntax: OnUnoccupiedVehicleUpdate(vehicleid, playerid, passenger_seat, new_x, new_y, new_z, vel_x, vel_y, vel_z)
member.param: The ID of the vehicle that's position was updated
The ID of the player that sent a vehicle position sync update
The ID of the seat if the player is a passenger. 0=not in vehicle, 1=front passenger, 2=backleft 3=backright 4+ is for coach/bus etc. with many passenger seats
Float The new X coordinate of the vehicle.
Float The new Y coordinate of the vehicle.
Float The new Z coordinate of the vehicle.
Float The new X velocity of the vehicle.
Float The new Y velocity of the vehicle.
Float The new Z velocity of the vehicle.
member.summary: This callback is called when a player's client updates/syncs the position of a vehicle they're not driving. This can happen outside of the vehicle or when the player is a passenger of a vehicle that has no driver.
member.remarks: This callback is called very frequently per second per unoccupied vehicle. You should refrain from implementing intensive calculations or intensive file writing/reading operations in this callback.
GetVehiclePos will return the old coordinates of the vehicle before this update.
member.returns:
Returning 0 in this callback will stop the vehicle's position being synced to other players. Update is still sent to the updating player. Useful for combating vehicle teleport hacks.
It is always called first in filterscripts so returning 0 there also blocks other scripts from seeing it.

member.remarks: This does not include vehicle health changes

1 - Will prevent other filterscripts from receiving this callback.

member.name: M:OnVehicleDeath
member.param: The ID of the vehicle that was destroyed
The ID of the player that reported (synced) the vehicle's destruction (name is misleading). Generally the driver or a passenger (if any) or the closest player
member.summary: This callback is called when a vehicle is destroyed - either by exploding or becoming submerged in water.
member.remarks: This callback can also be called by NPC.

This callback will also be called when a vehicle enters water, but the vehicle can be saved from destruction by teleportation or driving out (if only partially submerged). The callback won't be called a second time, and the vehicle may disappear when the driver exits, or after a short time.
member.returns:
This callback does not handle returns.
It is always called first in filterscripts.

The colour that the vehicle's secondary colour was changed to
member.summary: This callback is called when a player exits a mod shop, even if the colours weren't changed. Watch out, the name is ambiguous, Pay 'n' Spray shops don't call this callback.

member.param: The ID of the player to play the audio for
[] The url to play. Valid formats are mp3 and ogg/vorbis. A link to a .pls (playlist) file will play that playlist
Float The x position at which to play the audio. Has no effect unless usepos is set to 1 (optional=0.0)
Float The y position at which to play the audio. Has no effect unless usepos is set to 1 (optional=0.0)
Float The z position at which to play the audio. Has no effect unless usepos is set to 1 (optional=0.0)

bool Use the positions and distance specified. (optional=0)
member.summary: Play an 'audio stream' for a player. Normal audio files also work (e.g. MP3).
member.returns:
1: The function executed successfully.
0: The function failed to execute. The player specified does not exist.

member.name: M:PlayerPlaySound
member.param: The ID of the player for whom to play the sound
The sound to play
Float x coordinate for the sound to play at. (optional=0.0)
Float y coordinate for the sound to play at. (optional=0.0)
Float z coordinate for the sound to play at. (optional=0.0)
member.summary: Plays the specified sound for a player.
member.remarks: Only use the coordinates if you want the sound to be played at a certain position. By default, the sound will be played with no position at a certain position.
member.returns:
1: The function executed successfully.
0: The function failed to execute. This means the player is not connected.

member.name: M:PlayerSpectatePlayer
member.param: The ID of the player that will spectate
The ID of the player that should be spectated
t_SPECTATE_MODE The mode to spectate with (optional=SPECTATE_MODE_NORMAL)
member.summary: Makes a player spectate (watch) another player.
member.remarks: Order is CRITICAL! Ensure that you use TogglePlayerSpectating before PlayerSpectatePlayer.
playerid and targetid's virtual world and interior must be the same for this function to work properly.

ul.li: SPECTATE_MODE_NORMAL - normal spectate mode (third person point of view). Camera can not be changed.
SPECTATE_MODE_FIXED - use SetPlayerCameraPos after this to position the player's camera, and it will track the player/vehicle set with PlayerSpectatePlayer/PlayerSpectateVehicle.
SPECTATE_MODE_SIDE - the camera will be attached to the side of the player/vehicle (like when you're in first-person camera on a bike and you do a wheelie).

member.returns:
1: The function executed successfully.
0: The function failed to execute. One of the players specified does not exist.

member.name: M:PlayerSpectateVehicle
member.param: The ID of the player who should spectate a vehicle
The ID of the vehicle the player should spectate
t_SPECTATE_MODE The spectate mode. Can generally be left blank as it defaults to 'normal'
member.summary: Sets a player to spectate another vehicle. Their camera will be attached to the vehicle as if they are driving it.
member.remarks: Order is CRITICAL! Ensure that you use TogglePlayerSpectating before PlayerSpectatePlayer.
playerid and targetid have to be in the same interior for this function to work properly.
member.returns:
1: The function executed successfully. Note that success is reported if the player is not in spectator mode (TogglePlayerSpectating), but nothing will happen. TogglePlayerSpectating MUST be used first.
0: The function failed to execute. The player, vehicle, or both don't exist.

[] The new string for the TextDraw. May be optionally formatted.

member.name: M:PlayerTextDrawTextSize
member.param: The ID of the player whose player-textdraw to set the size of
PlayerText The ID of the player-textdraw to set the size of
Float The size on the x axis (left/right) following the same 640x480 grid as TextDrawCreate
Float The size on the y axis (up/down) following the same 640x480 grid as TextDrawCreate
member.summary: Change the size of a player-textdraw (box if PlayerTextDrawUseBox is enabled and/or clickable area for use with PlayerTextDrawSetSelectable).
member.remarks:
The x and y have different meanings with different PlayerTextDrawAlignment values:
1 (left): they are the right-most corner of the box, absolute coordinates.
2 (center): they need to inverted (switch the two) and the x value is the overall width of the box.
3 (right): the x and y are the coordinates of the left-most corner of the box

Using font type 4 (sprite) and 5 (model preview) converts x and y of this function from corner coordinates to WIDTH and HEIGHT (offsets).
The TextDraw box starts 10.0 units up and 5.0 to the left as the origin (TextDrawCreate coordinate).
This function defines the clickable area for use with PlayerTextDrawSetSelectable, whether a box is shown or not.

member.name: M:PlayerTextDrawUseBox

member.name: M:PutPlayerInVehicle

The ID of the vehicle to put the player in
The ID of the seat to put the player in
member.summary: Puts a player in a vehicle.
member.remarks: If this function is used on a player that is already in a vehicle, other players will still see them in their previous vehicle. To fix this, first remove the player from the vehicle.
If the seat is invalid or is taken, will cause a crash when they EXIT the vehicle.
You can use GetPlayerVehicleSeat in a loop to check if a seat is occupied by any players.
member.returns:
1: The function executed successfully.
0: The function failed to execute. The player or vehicle don't exist.

member.summary: Removes/ejects a player from their vehicle.
member.remarks:
The exiting animation is not synced for other players.
This function will not work when used in OnPlayerEnterVehicle, because the player isn't in the vehicle when the callback is called. Use OnPlayerStateChange instead.
The player isn't removed if he is in a RC Vehicle.
member.returns:
1: The function executed successfully.
0: The function failed to execute. This means the player is not connected.

member.name: M:SSCANF_GetClosestString
member.param: [] The first string to compare.
[][] A list of other strings to compare against.
How similar the strings must be to be considered a match.
The number of candidates.
member.remarks: Takes an input string and an array of string possibilities (candidates) and returns the index of the string closest to the input string. If no valid match is found, -1 is returned. Note that this will always return the closest, even if the closest is not that close; which is why an optional threshold parameter is available. When this parameter is provided the closest match must be closer in Levenshtein distance than the threshold, otherwise again -1 is returned.

member.name: M:SSCANF_GetClosestValue
member.syntax: SSCANF_GetClosestValue(input[], candidates[][], results[], fail, threshold, count, check)
member.param: [] The first string to compare.
[][] A list of other strings to compare against.
[] The values to return when the corresponding candidate (by index) is the closest match.
The value to return when there is no good match.
How similar the strings must be to be considered a match.
The number of candidates (must match check).
The number of results (must match count).
member.remarks: Similar to SSCANF_GetClosestString in that it searches the candidates array for the string most closely matching the input and bounded by threshold. But instead of returning the index this function returns the value in the second results array at that index; and instead of returning -1 on failure it returns the value of fail. The two arrays must match in size and an assert in the function checks for this.

member.name: M:SSCANF_GetSimilarString
member.param: [] The first string to compare.
[][] A list of other strings to compare against.
Float How similar the strings must be to be considered a match.
The number of candidates.
member.remarks: Takes an input string and an array of string possibilities (candidates) and returns the index of the string closest to the input string. If no valid match is found, -1 is returned. Note that this will always return the closest, even if the closest is not that close; which is why an optional threshold parameter is available. When this parameter is provided the closest match must be closer in Levenshtein distance than the threshold, otherwise again -1 is returned.

member.param: [] The first string to compare.
[][] A list of other strings to compare against.
[] The values to return when the corresponding candidate (by index) is the closest match.
The value to return when there is no good match.

The number of candidates (must match check).
The number of results (must match count).
member.remarks: Similar to SSCANF_GetClosestString in that it searches the candidates array for the string most closely matching the input and bounded by threshold. But instead of returning the index this function returns the value in the second results array at that index; and instead of returning -1 on failure it returns the value of fail. The two arrays must match in size and an assert in the function checks for this.

member.name: M:SSCANF_Levenshtein
member.param: [] The first string to compare.
[] The second string to compare.
member.remarks: Computes the Levenshtein Distance between two strings. This is simlar to strcmp in usage, but is slightly more "fuzzy". Distances are used to work out which string is the most similar to another one, though they may not be identical. Useful in k callback functions to determine if the entered string is close to a possible string.

member.name: M:SSCANF_Option
member.param: [] The sscanf option to get or set. For example SSCANF_ARGB.
{_,Float} The optional value to use when setting the option.
member.remarks:
Backwards-compatibility with SSCANF_Option. Usage: SSCANF_Option(SSCANF_QUIET, 1);
new quiet = SSCANF_Option(SSCANF_QUIET); This doesn't actually use a default parameter because a user may want to set the option to whatever that parameter is. Instead this is a macro that calls SSCANF_GetOption__ when called with one parameter (just a name), or instead calls SSCANF_SetOption__ when called with two - a name and a value.

member.name: M:SSCANF_RunInit
member.remarks: Generic initialisation code called from a range of different init publics.

member.name: M:SSCANF_TextSimilarity
member.param: [] The first string to compare.
[] The second string to compare.
member.remarks: This works out the similarity between two strings. The Levenshtein distance often produces results that seem weird to people, for example by that measure NRG is closer to TUG than NRG-500. Instead this function compares all pairs of letters between the two strings to work out what percentage of each string is in the other string, then multiplies the results to get the final similarity. This algorithm produces much more human sane results, and can handle things like ls police matching Police Car (LSPD). It ignores all punctuation and case as well.

member.name: M:SSCANF_Version
member.param: [] Pass-by-reference return value.
The size of the destination array.
member.remarks: Get the SSCANF plugin version as a string (e.g. "2.11.2") or a BCD-encoded value (e.g. 0x021102). This function returns a string (by reference) when called with parameters, or a number (directly) when not.

member.name: M:SSCANF__
member.param: [] The file in which this call is found.
The line at which this call is found.
[] The input string containing the data to parse out.
[] The format description of what the input data should contain.
member.remarks: The current true implementation of sscanf in the plugin. This is wrapped by macros to provide sscanf enhanced with filenames and line numbers so that errors have more information. The plugin also contains a native function called sscanf which is only for backwards- compatibility with older versions of this include.

member.name: M:SendClientMessage
member.param: The ID of the player to display the message to
The colour of the message (RGBA)
[] The text that will be displayed (max 144 characters). Optionally formatted.
member.summary: This function sends a message to a specific player with a chosen colour in the chat. The whole line in the chatbox will be in the set colour unless colour embedding is used (since 0.3c).
member.remarks: If a message is longer than 144 characters, it will not be sent. Truncation can be used to prevent this. Displaying a message on multiple lines will also solve this issue.
Avoid using the percent sign (or format specifiers) in the actual message text without properly escaping it (like %%). It will result in crashes otherwise.
You can use colour embedding for multiple colours in the message.
member.returns:
1: The function executed successfully. Success is reported when the string is over 144 characters, but the message won't be sent.
0: The function failed to execute. The player is not connected.

member.name: M:SendClientMessageToAll
member.param: The colour of the message (RGBA)
[] The message to show (max 144 characters). Optionally formatted.
member.summary: Displays a message in chat to all players. This is a multi-player equivalent of SendClientMessage.
member.remarks: Avoid using format specifiers in your messages without formatting the string that is sent. It will result in crashes otherwise.
member.returns: This function always returns 1.

member.remarks: Does not support login, due to the lack of a 'playerid' parameter.
'password 0' will remove the server's password if one is set.
member.returns: This function always returns 1.
member.remarks: This function will result in OnRconCommand being called.

member.name: M:SetPlayerCheckpoint
member.param: The ID of the player for whom to set a checkpoint
Float The x coordinate to set the checkpoint at
Float The y coordinate to set the checkpoint at
Float The z coordinate to set the checkpoint at
Float The radius of the checkpoint
member.summary: Sets a checkpoint (red cylinder) for a player. Also shows a red blip on the radar. When players enter a checkpoint, OnPlayerEnterCheckpoint is called and actions can be performed.
member.remarks:
If a checkpoint is already set it will use the radius of that checkpoint instead of the new one.
Checkpoints created on server-created objects (CreateObject/ CreatePlayerObject) will appear down on the 'real' ground, but will still function correctly. A pickup can be used instead.
Checkpoints are asynchronous, meaning only one can be shown at a time. To 'stream' checkpoints (only show them when players are close enough), use a checkpoint streamer.
member.returns:
1: The function executed successfully.
0: The function failed to execute. This means the player specified does not exist.

member.name: M:SetPlayerColor
member.param: The ID of the player whose colour to set
The colour to set. Supports alpha values (RGBA)
member.summary: Set the colour of a player's nametag and marker (radar blip).
member.remarks: This function will change player's colour for everyone, even if player's colour was changed with SetPlayerMarkerForPlayer for any other player.
If used under OnPlayerConnect, the affecting player will not see the colour in the TAB menu.

member.name: M:SetPlayerDrunkLevel
member.param: The ID of the player to set the drunkenness of
The level of drunkenness to set
member.summary: Sets the drunk level of a player which makes the player's camera sway and vehicles hard to control.
member.remarks:
Players' drunk level will automatically decrease over time, based on their FPS (players with 50 FPS will lose 50 'levels' per second. This is useful for determining a player's FPS!).
In 0.3a the drunk level will decrement and stop at 2000. In 0.3b+ the drunk level decrements to 0)
Levels over 2000 make the player drunk (camera swaying and vehicles difficult to control).
Max drunk level is 50000.
While the drunk level is above 5000, the player's HUD (radar etc.) will be hidden.
member.returns:
1: The function executed successfully.
0: The function failed to execute. This means the player is not connected.

member.name: M:SetPlayerFacingAngle
member.param: The ID of the player to set the facing angle of
Float The angle the player should face
member.summary: Set a player's facing angle (z rotation).
member.remarks: Angles are reversed in GTA:SA; 90 degrees would be East in the real world, but in GTA:SA 90 degrees is in fact West. North and South are still 0/360 and 180. To convert this, simply do 360 - angle.
member.returns:
1: The function executed successfully.
0: The function failed to execute. The player specified does not exist.

member.remarks: This does not affect normal fist attacks - only special/secondary attacks (aim + press 'secondary attack' key).

member.name: M:SetPlayerMapIcon
member.syntax: SetPlayerMapIcon(playerid, iconid, x, y, z, markerType, colour, style)
member.param: The ID of the player to set the map icon for
The player's icon ID, ranging from 0 to 99. This means there is a maximum of 100 map icons. ID can be used in RemovePlayerMapIcon
Float The x coordinate to place the map icon at
Float The y coordinate to place the map icon at
Float The z coordinate to place the map icon at
The icon to set
The colour of the icon (RGBA). This should only be used with the square icon (ID: 0)
t_MAPICON The style of icon (optional=MAPICON_LOCAL)
member.summary: Place an icon/marker on a player's map. Can be used to mark locations such as banks and hospitals to players.
member.remarks: If you use an invalid marker type, it will create ID 1 (White Square).
If you use an icon ID that is already in use, it will replace the current map icon using that ID.
Marker type 1 (square), 2 (player blip), 4 (north), and 56 (single airstrip blip) will cause your game to crash if you have map legends enabled while viewing the map.

ul.li: 1 MAPICON_GLOBAL - show on radar edge as long as in range
3 MAPICON_GLOBAL_CHECKPOINT - Show on radar edge as long as in range (with checkpoint)

member.returns:
1: The function executed successfully.
0: The function failed to execute. Player is not connected.

member.name: M:SetPlayerName
member.param: The ID of the player to set the name of
[] The name to set. Must be 1-24 characters long and only contain valid characters (0-9, a-z, A-z, [], (), $, @, ., _, =)
member.summary: Sets the name of a player.
member.remarks: Changing the players' name to the same name but with different character cases (e.g. "John" to "JOHN") will not work.
If used in OnPlayerConnect, the new name will not be shown for the connecting player.
Passing a null string as the new name will crash the server.

Player names can be up to 24 characters when using this function, but when joining the server from the server browser, players' names must be no more than 20 and less than 3 characters (the server will deny entry). This allows for 4 characters extra when using SetPlayerName.
member.returns:
1 The name was changed successfully.
0 The player is not connected or the name is already in use.
-1 The name can not be changed (it's less than 3 symbols, too long or has invalid characters).

member.name: M:SetPlayerSkin
member.param: The ID of the player to set the skin of
The skin the player should use
member.summary: Set the skin of a player. A player's skin is their character model.
member.remarks: If a player's skin is set when they are crouching, in a vehicle, or performing certain animations, they will become frozen or otherwise glitched. This can be fixed by using TogglePlayerControllable. Players can be detected as being crouched through GetPlayerSpecialAction (SPECIAL_ACTION_DUCK).
Setting a player's skin when he is dead may crash players around him.
Note that 'success' is reported even when skin ID is invalid (not 0-311, or 74), but the skin will be set to ID 0 (CJ).
member.returns:
1: The function executed successfully.
0: The function failed to execute. This means the player specified does not exist.

member.name: M:SetPlayerSpecialAction
member.param: The player that should perform the action
t_SPECIAL_ACTION The action that should be performed
member.summary: This function allows to set players special action.
member.remarks: Removing jetpacks from players by setting their special action to 0 causes the sound to stay until death.

remarks.b: Special actions: (marked with * cannot be set)

ul.li: 1 - SPECIAL_ACTION_DUCK * - Detect if the player is crouching.
3 - SPECIAL_ACTION_ENTER_VEHICLE * - Detect if the player is entering a vehicle via an animation.
4 - SPECIAL_ACTION_EXIT_VEHICLE * - Detect if the player is exiting a vehicle via an animation.
20 - SPECIAL_ACTION_DRINK_BEER - Will increase the player's drunk level when used
21 - SPECIAL_ACTION_SMOKE_CIGGY - Will give the player a cigar
22 - SPECIAL_ACTION_DRINK_WINE - Will give the player a wine bottle to get drunk from
23 - SPECIAL_ACTION_DRINK_SPRUNK - Will give the player a sprunk bottle to drink from
24 - SPECIAL_ACTION_CUFFED - Will force the player in to cuffs (hands are behind their back) (does not work on CJ skin)
25 - SPECIAL_ACTION_CARRY - Will apply a 'carrying' animation to the player and make them unable to sprint, jump or punch (does not work on CJ skin)
68 - SPECIAL_ACTION_PISSING - Will make make the player perform the pissing animation with visible pee.

member.returns:
1: The function executed successfully.
0: The function failed to execute. This means the player is not connected.

member.name: M:SetPlayerTeam
member.param: The ID of the player you want to set the team of
The team to put the player in. Use NO_TEAM to remove the player from any team
member.summary: Set the team of a player.
member.remarks: Players can not damage/kill players on the same team unless they use a knife to slit their throat. Players are also unable to damage vehicles driven by a player from the same team. This can be enabled with EnableVehicleFriendlyFire.
255 (or NO_TEAM) is the default team to be able to shoot other players, not 0.

member.returns:
