0: The function failed to execute. This means the player is not connected.

member.name: M:SetPlayerWeather
member.param: The ID of the player whose weather to set
The weather to set
member.summary: Set a player's weather.
member.remarks: If TogglePlayerClock is enabled, weather will slowly change over time, instead of changing instantly.

member.name: M:SetSpawnInfo
member.syntax: SetSpawnInfo(playerid, team, skin, spawnX, spawnY, spawnZ, angle, weapon1, ammo1, weapon2, ammo2, weapon3, ammo3)
member.param: The Playerid of who you want to set the spawn information
The Team-ID of the chosen player
The skin which the player will spawn with
Float The x coordinate of the player's spawn position
Float The y coordinate of the player's spawn position
Float The z coordinate of the player's spawn position
Float The direction in which the player should face after spawning
t_WEAPON The first spawn-weapon for the player
The amount of ammunition for the primary spawnweapon
t_WEAPON The second spawn-weapon for the player
The amount of ammunition for the second spawnweapon

The amount of ammunition for the third spawnweapon
member.summary: This function can be used to change the spawn information of a specific player. It allows you to automatically set someone's spawn weapons, their team, skin and spawn position, normally used in case of minigames or automatic-spawn systems.

member.name: M:SetTeamCount
member.param: Number of teams the gamemode knows
member.summary: This function is used to change the amount of teams used in the gamemode. It has no obvious way of being used, but can help to indicate the number of teams used for better (more effective) internal handling. This function should only be used in the OnGameModeInit callback. Important: You can pass 2 billion here if you like, this function has no effect at all.

member.name: M:SetTimer
member.param: [] Name of the function to call as a string. This must be a public function (forwarded). A null string here will crash the server
Interval in milliseconds
bool Whether the timer should repeat or not
member.summary: Sets a 'timer' to call a function after some time. Can be set to repeat.
member.remarks: Timer IDs are never used twice. You can use KillTimer on a timer ID and it won't matter if it's running or not.
The function that should be called must be public.
The use of many timers will result in increased memory/cpu usage.
member.returns: The ID of the timer that was started. Timer IDs start at 1.

member.name: M:SetTimerEx
member.param: [] The name of a public function to call when the timer expires
Interval in milliseconds
bool Whether the timer should be called repeatedly (can only be stopped with KillTimer) or only once
[] Special format indicating the types of values the timer will pass

{Bit,Bitmap,XML,XMLEntry,Group,INI,Language,Style,DB,DBResult,Menu,Text,PlayerText,Text3D,PlayerText3D,File,Float,CUSTOM_TAG_TYPES,_} Indefinite number of arguments to pass (must follow format specified in previous parameter)
member.summary: Sets a timer to call a function after the specified interval. This variant ('Ex') can pass parameters (such as a player ID) to the function.
member.remarks: Timer IDs are never used twice. You can use KillTimer() on a timer ID and it won't matter if it's running or not.
The function that should be called must be public.
The use of many timers will result in increased memory/cpu usage.

ul.li: a - array The next parameter must be an integer ("i") with the array's size

member.returns: The ID of the timer that was started. Timer IDs start at 1 and are never reused. There are no internal checks to verify that the parameters passed are valid (e.g. duration not a minus value).

member.name: M:SetVehicleHealth
member.param: The ID of the vehicle to set the health of
Float The health, given as a float value
member.summary: Set a vehicle's health. When a vehicle's health decreases the engine will produce smoke, and finally fire when it decreases to less than 250 (25%).
member.remarks: Full vehicle health is 1000, however higher values are possible and increase the health of the vehicle.
member.returns:
1: The function executed successfully.
0: The function failed to execute. This means the vehicle does not exist.

member.name: M:SetVehicleNumberPlate
member.param: The ID of the vehicle to set the number plate of
[] The text that should be displayed on the number plate
member.summary: Set a vehicle numberPlate.
member.remarks: You can use colour embedding on the number plate text.

This function has no internal error checking. Do not assign custom number plates to vehicles without plates (boats, planes, etc) as this will result in some unneeded processing time on the client.
The vehicle must be re-spawned or re-streamed for the changes to take effect.
There's a limit of 32 characters on each number plate (including embedded colours).
The text length that can be seen on the number plate is around 9 to 10 characters, more characters will cause the text to split.
Some vehicle models has a backward number plate, e.g. Boxville (498) (as an alternative to this vehicle you can use vehicle model ID 609, which is a duplicated Boxville (aka Boxburg), but with a regular number plate).
member.returns:
1: The function executed successfully.
0: The function failed to execute. The vehicle does not exist.

member.name: M:SetVehicleParamsEx
member.syntax: SetVehicleParamsEx(vehicleid, engine, lights, alarm, doors, bonnet, boot, objective)
member.param: The ID of the vehicle to set the parameters of
Engine status. 0 - Off, 1 - On
Light status. 0 - Off, 1 - On
Vehicle alarm status. If on, the alarm starts. 0 - Off, 1 - On
Door lock status. 0 - Unlocked, 1 - Locked
Bonnet (hood) status. 0 - Closed, 1 - Open
Boot/trunk status. 0 - Closed, 1 - Open
Toggle the objective arrow above the vehicle. 0 - Off, 1 - On
member.summary: Sets a vehicle's parameters for all players.
member.remarks: The alarm will not reset when finished, you'll need to reset it by yourself with this function.
Lights also operate during the day (Only when ManualVehicleEngineAndLights is enabled).
member.returns:
1: The function executed successfully.
0: The function failed to execute. This means the vehicle does not exist.

member.name: M:SetVehicleParamsForPlayer
member.param: The ID of the player to set the vehicle's parameters for
0 to disable the objective or 1 to show it. This is a bobbing yellow arrow above the vehicle
0 to unlock the doors or 1 to lock them
member.summary: Set the parameters of a vehicle for a player.
member.param: The ID of the vehicle to set the parameters of
member.remarks: Vehicles must be respawned for the 'objective' to be removed. This can be circumvented somewhat using Get/SetVehicleParamsEx which do not require the vehicle to be respawned. It is worth noting however that the object will be disabled on a global scale, and this is only useful if only one player has the vehicle as an objective.
Since 0.3a you will have to reapply this function when OnVehicleStreamIn is called.
member.returns:
1: The function executed successfully.
0: The function failed to execute. The player and/or vehicle specified do not exist.

member.name: M:TextDrawAlignment

t_TEXT_DRAW_ALIGN 1-left 2-centered 3-right
member.summary: Set the alignment of text in a text draw.

member.name: M:TextDrawBackgroundColour
member.param: Text The ID of the textdraw to set the background colour of
The colour that the textdraw should be set to
member.summary: Adjusts the text draw area background colour (the outline/shadow - NOT the box. For box colour, see TextDrawBoxColour).
member.remarks: If TextDrawSetOutline is used with size > 0, the outline colour will match the colour used in TextDrawBackgroundColour. Changing the value of colour seems to alter the colour used in TextDrawColour

If you want to change the background colour of a textdraw that is already shown, you don't have to recreate it. Simply use TextDrawShowForPlayer/ TextDrawShowForAll after modifying the textdraw and the change will be visible.

member.name: M:TextDrawBoxColour
member.param: Text The TextDraw to change
The colour (RGBA)
member.summary: Adjusts the text box colour (only used if TextDrawUseBox 'use' parameter is 1).
member.remarks: If you want to change the boxcolour of a textdraw that is already shown, you don't have to recreate it. Simply use TextDrawShowForPlayer/ TextDrawShowForAll after modifying the textdraw and the change will be visible.

member.name: M:TextDrawColour
member.param: Text The ID of the textdraw to change the colour of.
The colour to set the textdraw to
member.summary: Sets the text colour of a textdraw.
member.remarks: You can also use GameText colours in TextDraws.
If the TextDraw is already shown, it must be re-shown (TextDrawShowForAll/TextDrawShowForPlayer) for the changes of this function to take effect.

member.name: M:TextDrawCreate
member.param: Float The x (left/right) coordinate to create the textdraw at
Float The y (up/down) coordinate to create the textdraw at
[] The text that will appear in the textdraw. May be optionally formatted.
member.summary: Creates a textdraw. Textdraws are, as the name implies, text (mainly - there can be boxes, sprites and model previews (skins/vehicles/weapons/objects too) that is drawn on a player's screens.
member.remarks:
If you choose values for y that are less than 1, the first text row will be invisible and only the shadow is visible.
If part of the text is off-screen, the colour of the text will not show, only the shadow (if enabled) will.

Maximum length of textdraw is 800 characters. Longer text will crash the legacy (SA:MP) client in older versions.
If you use colour codes (such as ~R~ ~G~) beyond 255th character the legacy (SA:MP) client will crash trying to display the textdraw.

The x, y coordinate is the top left coordinate for the text draw area based on a 640x480 "canvas" (irrespective of screen resolution). If you plan on using TextDrawAlignment with alignment 3 (right), the x, y coordinate is the top right coordinate for the text draw.
This function merely CREATES the textdraw, you must use TextDrawShowForPlayer or TextDrawShowForAll to show it.
It is recommended to use WHOLE numbers instead of decimal positions when creating textdraws to ensure resolution friendly design.
Keyboard key mapping codes (such as ~k~~VEHICLE_ENTER_EXIT~ don't work beyond 255th character.
member.returns: The ID of the created textdraw. Textdraw IDs start at 0.

member.name: M:TextDrawFont
member.param: Text The TextDraw to change

t_TEXT_DRAW_FONT There are four font styles, see https://open.mp/docs/scripting/functions/TextDrawFont. Font value 4 specifies that this is a txd sprite; 5 specifies that this textdraw can display preview models. A font value greater than 5 does not display, and anything greater than 16 crashes the client
member.summary: Changes the text font.
member.remarks: If you want to change the font of a textdraw that is already shown, you don't have to recreate it. Simply use TextDrawShowForPlayer/TextDrawShowForAll after modifying the textdraw and the change will be visible.

member.name: M:TextDrawHideForPlayer
member.param: The ID of the player that the textdraw should be hidden for
Text The ID of the textdraw to hide
member.summary: Hides a textdraw for a specific player.

member.name: M:TextDrawLetterSize
member.param: Text The TextDraw to change
Float Width of a char
Float Height of a char
member.summary: Sets the width and height of the letters.
member.remarks: When using this function purely for the benefit of affecting the TextDraw box, multiply 'y' by 0.135 to convert to TextDrawTextSize-like measurements. Hint: it is easier and extremely precise to use LD_SPAC:white sprite for box-only textdraws, TextDrawTextSize will have regular offsets.

If you want to change the letter size of a textdraw that is already shown, you don't have to recreate it. Simply use TextDrawShowForPlayer/ TextDrawShowForAll after modifying the textdraw and the change will be visible.
Fonts appear to look the best with an x to y ratio of 1 to 4 (e.g. if x is 0.5 then y should be 2).

member.name: M:TextDrawSetShadow
member.param: Text The ID of the textdraw to set the shadow size of
The size of the shadow. 1 is generally used for a normal shadow size. 0 disables the shadow completely
member.summary: Sets the size of a textdraw's text's shadow.
member.remarks: The shadow can be cut by the box area if the size is set too big for the area.

If you want to change the shadow of a textdraw that is already shown, you don't have to recreate it. Simply use TextDrawShowForPlayer/TextDrawShowForAll after modifying the textdraw and the change will be visible.
member.returns:
1: The function executed successfully.
0: The function failed to execute. The textdraw does not exist.

member.name: M:TextDrawSetString
member.param: Text The TextDraw to change
[] The new string for the TextDraw. May be optionally formatted.
member.summary: Changes the text on a textdraw.
member.remarks: There are limits to the length of textdraw strings - see here for more info.

member.name: M:TextDrawShowForPlayer
member.param: The ID of the player to show the textdraw for
Text The ID of the textdraw to show. Returned by TextDrawCreate
member.summary: Shows a textdraw for a specific player.
member.remarks: If only a single player will see a textdraw, it might be wise to use player-textdraws instead. This is also useful for textdraws that need to show information specific for an individual player.
member.returns:
1: The function executed successfully.
0: The function failed to execute. This means either the player and/or textdraw specified does not exist.

member.name: M:TextDrawTextSize
member.param: Text The TextDraw to set the size of
Float The size on the x axis (left/right) following the same 640x480 grid as TextDrawCreate
Float The size on the y axis (up/down) following the same 640x480 grid as TextDrawCreate
member.summary: Change the size of a textdraw (box if TextDrawUseBox is enabled and/or clickable area for use with TextDrawSetSelectable).
member.remarks:
The x and y have different meanings with different TextDrawAlignment values:
1 (left): they are the right-most corner of the box, absolute coordinates.
2 (center): they need to inverted (switch the two) and the x value is the overall width of the box.
3 (right): the x and y are the coordinates of the left-most corner of the box

Using font type 4 (sprite) and 5 (model preview) converts x and y of this function from corner coordinates to WIDTH and HEIGHT (offsets).
The TextDraw box starts 10.0 units up and 5.0 to the left as the origin (TextDrawCreate coordinate).
This function defines the clickable area for use with TextDrawSetSelectable, whether a box is shown or not.

If you want to change the text size of a textdraw that is already shown, you don't have to recreate it. Simply use TextDrawShowForPlayer/ TextDrawShowForAll after modifying the textdraw and the change will be visible.

member.name: M:TextDrawUseBox
member.param: Text The ID of the text textdraw to toggle the box of
bool 1 to show a box or 0 to not show a box
member.summary: Toggle whether a textdraw uses a box or not.
member.remarks: If the textdraw is already shown, it must be re-shown (TextDrawShowForAll/TextDrawShowForPlayer) to show the changes of this function.
member.returns:
1: The function executed successfully.
0: The function failed to execute. This means the textdraw specified does not exist.

member.name: M:TogglePlayerControllable
member.param: The ID of the player to toggle the controllability of
member.summary: Toggles whether a player can control their character or not. The player will also be unable to move their camera.
member.param: 0 to make them uncontrollable, 1 to make them controllable
member.returns:
1: The function executed successfully.
0: The function failed to execute. The player specified does not exist.

member.name: M:TogglePlayerSpectating
member.param: The ID of the player who should spectate
bool 1 to enable spectating and 0 to disable
member.summary: Toggle whether a player is in spectator mode or not. While in spectator mode a player can spectate (watch) other players and vehicles. After using this function, either PlayerSpectatePlayer or PlayerSpectateVehicle needs to be used.
member.remarks: If the player is not loaded in before setting the spectate status to false, the connection can be closed unexpectedly.

When spectator mode is disabled, OnPlayerSpawn will automatically be called, if you wish to restore player to state before spectating, you will have to handle that in OnPlayerSpawn. Note also, that player can also go to class selection before if they used F4 during spectate, a player also CAN die in spectate mode due to various glitches.

When a player is in spectate mode their HUD is hidden, making it useful for setting a player's camera without the HUD. Also, objects near the player's camera will be streamed in, making this useful for interpolating cameras.
member.returns:
1: The function executed successfully.
0: The function failed to execute. The player does not exist.

t_VEHICLE_TYRE_STATUS A set of bits containing the tyre damage status

member.name: M:_SSCANF_IncludeStates
member.remarks: Common ALS boilerplate to ensure that all the <_ALS : > states are known to the compiler.

member.name: M:__printf
member.param: [] The format string
{Bit,Bitmap,XML,XMLEntry,Group,INI,Language,Style,DB,DBResult,Menu,Text,PlayerText,Text3D,PlayerText3D,File,Float,CUSTOM_TAG_TYPES,_} Indefinite number of arguments of any tag
member.summary: Outputs a formatted string on the console (the server window, not the in-game chat).
member.remarks: The format string or its output should not exceed 1024 characters. Anything beyond that length can lead to a server to crash.
This function doesn't support packed strings.

ul.li: %q - escape a text for SQLite. (Added in 0.3.7 R2)

member.remarks: The values for the placeholders follow in the exact same order as parameters in the call. For example, "I am %i years old" - the %i will be replaced with an Integer variable, which is the person's age.

You may optionally put a number between the % and the letter of the placeholder code. This number indicates the field width; if the size of the parameter to print at the position of the placeholder is smaller than the field width, the field is expanded with spaces. To cut the number of decimal places beeing shown of a float, you can add .<max number> between the % and the f. (example: %.2f)

member.name: M:clreol
member.summary: Clear the rest of the line.
member.remarks: Clears the line at which the cursor is, from the position of the cursor to the right margin of the console. This function does not move the cursor.

member.name: M:clrscr
member.summary: Clear the screen.
member.remarks: Clears the display and moves the cursor to the upper left corner.

member.name: M:cvttimestamp
member.summary: Convert a time-stamp into a date and time.
member.param: This will hold the year upon return.
This will hold the month (1-12) upon return.
This will hold the day of (1-31) the month upon return.
This will hold the hour (0-23) upon return.
This will hold the minute (0-59) upon return.
This will hold the second (0-59) upon return.
member.remarks: Some file and system functions return time-stamps as the number of seconds since midnight, 1 January 1970, which is the start of the UNIX system epoch. This function allows to convert these time stamps into date and time fields. cvttimestamp = ConVerT TIMESTAMP.
member.returns: This function always returns 0.

member.name: M:delay
member.summary: Halts execution a number of milliseconds.
member.param: The delay, in milliseconds.
member.remarks: On some platforms, the sleep instruction also delays for a given number of milliseconds. The difference between the sleep instruction and the delay function is that the delay function does not yield events and the sleep instruction typically yields. When yielding events, any pending events are handled. As a result, the delay function waits without handling any pending events and the sleep instruction waits and deals with events.
member.returns: This function always returns 0.

member.name: M:diskfree
member.param: [] The name of the volume on systems that support multiple disks or multiple memory cards. On single-volume systems, it is optional.
member.summary: Returns the free disk space.
member.remarks: The maximum size that can be supported 2048 GiB (2 terabyte).
member.returns: The amount of free space in KiB.

member.name: M:floatround
member.param: Float The value to round
floatround_method The floatround method to use (optional=floatround_round)
member.summary: Round a floating point number to an integer value.

ul.li: floatround_round - round to the nearest integer. A fractional part of exactly 0.5 rounds upwards (this is the default).
floatround_tozero - round downwards for positive values and upwards for negative values ("truncate").

member.returns: The rounded value as an integer.

member.name: M:floatsqroot
member.param: Float The value to calculate the square root of
member.summary: Calculates the square root of given value.
member.remarks: This function raises a "domain" error if the input value is negative. You may use floatabs to get the absolute (positive) value.
member.returns: The square root of the input value, as a float.

member.name: M:floatstr
member.param: [] The string to convert into a float
member.summary: Converts a string to a float.
member.returns: The requested float value.

member.name: M:floatsub
member.param: Float First Float
Float Second Float (gets subtracted from the first float.)
member.summary: Subtracts one float from another one. Note that this function has no real use, as one can simply use the standard operator (-) instead.
member.returns: The difference of the two given floats.

member.name: M:fopen
member.param: [] The path to the file to open (if just a filename is specified, it will open the file with the name specified in the 'scriptfiles' folder)
filemode The mode to open the file with, see below (optional=io_readwrite)
member.summary: Open a file (to read from or write to).
member.remarks: This function can't access files outside the 'scriptfiles' folder!
If you use io_read and the file doesn't exist, it will return a NULL reference. Using invalid references on file functions will crash your server!

ul.li: io_write - write in the file, or create the file if it does not exist. Erases all existing contents.
io_readwrite - reads the file or creates it if it doesn't already exist.
io_append - appends (adds) to file, write-only. If the file does not exist, it is created.

member.returns: Returns the file handle. This handle is used for reading and writing. 0 if failed to open file.

member.name: M:format
member.param: [] The string to output the result to
The maximum length output can contain
[] The format string
{Float,_} Indefinite number of arguments of any tag
member.summary: Formats a string to include variables and other strings inside it.
member.remarks: This function doesn't support packed strings.

ul.li: %q - escape a text for SQLite. (Added in 0.3.7 R2)

member.remarks: The values for the placeholders follow in the exact same order as parameters in the call. For example, "I am %i years old" - the %i will be replaced with an Integer variable, which is the person's age.

You may optionally put a number between the % and the letter of the placeholder code. This number indicates the field width; if the size of the parameter to print at the position of the placeholder is smaller than the field width, the field is expanded with spaces. To cut the number of decimal places beeing shown of a float, you can add .<max number> between the % and the f. (example: %.2f)

member.name: M:fread
member.param: File The handle of the file to read from (returned by fopen)
[] A string array to store the read text in, passed by reference
The number of bytes to read (optional=sizeof (string))
bool Should the string be packed? (optional=false)
member.summary: Read a single line from a file.
member.remarks: Using an invalid handle will crash your server! Get a valid handle by using fopen or ftemp.
member.returns: The length of string (the read text) as an integer.

member.name: M:fwrite
member.param: File The handle of the file to write to (returned by fopen)
[] The string of text to write in to the file
member.summary: Write text into a file.
member.remarks: Using an invalid handle will crash your server! Get a valid handle by using fopen or ftemp.
This functions writes to the file in UTF-8, which does not support some localized language symbols.
This function doesn't support packed strings.
member.returns: The length of the written string as an integer.

member.name: M:getchar
member.param: bool If true, the character read from the keyboard is echoed on the display.
member.summary: Read one character.
member.return: The numeric code for the character that is read (this is usually the ASCII code).

member.name: M:getdate
member.param: & The variable to store the year in, passed by reference (optional=0)
& The variable to store the month in, passed by reference (optional=0)
& The variable to store the day in, passed by reference (optional=0)
member.summary: Get the current server date.
member.returns: The number of days since the start of the year.

member.name: M:getstring
member.summary: Read a line.
member.return: The number of characters read, excluding the terminating null character.
member.remarks: Function getstring stops reading when either the enter key is typed or the maximum length is reached. The maximum length is in cells (not characters) and it includes a terminating null character. The function can read both packed and unpacked strings; when reading a packed string, the function may read more characters than the size parameter specifies, since each cell holds multiple characters.

member.name: M:gettime
member.param: & The variable to store the hour in, passed by reference (optional=0)
& The variable to store the minute in, passed by reference (optional=0)
& The variable to store the seconds in, passed by reference (optional=0)
member.summary: Get the current server time.
member.returns: The function itself returns a Unix Timestamp.

member.name: M:getvalue
member.param: Must be between 2 and 36, use 10 for decimal or 16 for hexadecimal.
The character code that terminates the input. More than one character may be listed.
member.summary: Read a number.
member.remarks: Read a value (a signed number) from the keyboard. The getvalue function allows you to read in a numeric radix from 2 to 36 (the base parameter) with decimal radix by default. By default the input ends when the user types the enter key, but one or more different keys may be selected (the end parameter and subsequent). In the list of terminating keys, a positive number (like '\r') displays the key and terminates input, and a negative number terminates input without displaying the terminating key.

member.name: M:gotoxy
member.param: The new cursor position.
member.summary: Set cursor location.
member.return: true if the cursor moved and false if the requested position is invalid.
member.remarks: Sets the cursor position on the console. The upper left corner is at (1,1).

member.name: M:print
member.summary: Prints a string to the server console (not in-game chat) and logs (server_log.txt).
member.param: The string to print
The string to print

Colour codes for the foreground and the background of the text string; see function setattr for a list of colours. When left at -1, the default colours are used. Note that a terminal or a host application may not support colours.

member.name: M:random
member.param: The range of values (from 0 to max-1) that can be returned
member.summary: Get a pseudo-random number.
member.remarks: Using a value smaller than 1 gives weird values.

The standard random number generator of pawn is likely a linear congruential pseudo-random number generator with a range and a period of 2^31. Linear congruential pseudo-random number generators suffer from serial correlation (especially in the low bits) and it is unsuitable for applications that require high-quality random numbers.
member.returns: A random number ranging from 0 to max-1.

member.name: M:setattr
member.param: The string to print

Colour codes for the foreground and the background of the text string; see function setattr for a list of colours. When left at -1, the default colours are used. Note that a terminal or a host application may not support colours
member.summary: Set text colours.
member.remarks: On most systems, the colour value must be a value between zero and seven, as per the ANSI escape sequences, ISO 6429. Predefined constants for the colours are black (0), red (1), green (2), yellow (3), blue (4), magenta (5), cyan (6), and white (7).

member.name: M:setdate
member.param: The year to set; if set to "cellmin", the default value, it is ignored.
The month to set; if set to "cellmin", the default value, it is ignored.
The day to set; if set to "cellmin", the default value, it is ignored.
member.summary: Set the system date.
member.returns: This function always returns 0.
member.remarks: The date fields are kept in a valid range. For example, when setting the month to 13, it wraps back to 1 - this is always wrapped to the min/max, not modulo.

member.name: M:settime
member.param: The hour to set, in the range 0-23; if set to "cellmin", the default value, it is ignored.
The minute to set, in the range 0-59; if set to "cellmin", the default value, it is ignored.
The second to set, in the range 0-59; if set to "cellmin", the default value, it is ignored.
member.summary: Set the system time.
member.returns: This function always returns 0.
member.remarks: The time fields are kept in a valid range. For example, when setting the hour to 24, it wraps back to 0 - this is always wrapped to the min/max, not modulo.

member.name: M:settimer
member.summary: Configure the event timer.
member.param: The number of milliseconds to wait before calling the @timer callback function. If the timer is repetitive, this is the interval. When this parameter is 0 (zero), the timer is shut off.
If false, the timer is a repetitive timer; if true the timer is shut off after invoking the @timer event once.
member.remarks: See the chapter "Usage" for an example of this function, and the @timer event function.
member.returns: This function always returns 0.

member.name: M:settimestamp
member.summary: Sets the date and time with a single value.
member.param: The number of seconds that have elapsed since 00:00 hours, 1 January 1970. This particular date, 1 January 1970, is the UNIX system epoch.
member.returns: This function always returns 0.
member.remarks: The function getdate returns the number of seconds since 1 January 1970.

member.name: M:sscanf
member.param: [] The input string containing the data to parse out.
[] The format description of what the input data should contain.
member.remarks: The main entry point. See the readme for vast amounts of information on how to call this function and all the details on what it does. This is a macro that calls SSCANF__ and passes the current file and line number as well for improved error messages.

member.name: M:sscanf_vehicle
member.param: [] The current word being parsed out of the sscanf input.
member.remarks: The default implementation of k<vehicle>. Finds the closest vehicle by Levenshtein distance to the input.

member.name: M:sscanf_weapon
member.param: [] The current word being parsed out of the sscanf input.
member.remarks: The default implementation of k<weapon>. Finds the closest weapon by Levenshtein distance to the input.

member.name: M:strcat
member.param: [] The string to store the two concatenated strings in
[] The source string
The maximum length of the destination (optional=sizeof (dest))
member.summary: This function concatenates (joins together) two strings into the destination string.
member.returns: The length of the new destination string.

member.name: M:strcmp
member.param: [] The first string to compare
[] The second string to compare
bool When set to true, the case doesn't matter - HeLLo is the same as Hello. When false, they're not the same (optional=0)
When this length is set, the first x chars will be compared - doing "Hello" and "Hell No" with a length of 4 will say it's the same string (optional=cellmax)
member.summary: Compares two strings to see if they are the same.
member.remarks: This function returns 0 if either string is empty. Check for null strings with isnull(). If you do not, for example, people can login to anyone's account by simply entering a blank password.
If you compare strings from a text file, you should take in to account the 'carriage return' and 'new line' special characters (\r \n), as they are included, when using fread.
member.returns:
0 if strings match each other on given length;.
1 or -1 if some character do not match: string1[i] - string2[i].
difference in number of characters if one string matches only part of another string.

member.name: M:strcopy
member.summary: Copies a string into the destination string.
member.param: The string to copy the source string into
The source string
The maximum length of the destination (optional=sizeof (dest))
member.returns: The length of the new destination string.

member.name: M:strdel
member.param: [] The string to delete part of
The position of the first character to delete
The position of the last character to delete
member.summary: Delete part of a string.

member.name: M:strequal
member.summary: Compares two strings to see if they are the same.
member.param: The first string to compare
The second string to compare
When set to true, the case doesn't matter - HeLLo is the same as Hello. When false, they're not the same (optional=0)
When this length is set, the first x chars will be compared - doing "Hello" and "Hell No" with a length of 4 will say it's the same string (optional=cellmax)
member.remarks: This is a conveniece function that depends on strcmp.
member.returns: true if the strings match each other on given length, false otherwise.

member.name: M:strfind
member.param: [] The string you want to search in (haystack)
[] The string you want to search for (needle)
bool When set to true, the case doesn't matter - HeLLo is the same as Hello. When false, they're not the same (optional=0)
The offset to start searching from (optional=0)
member.summary: Search for a sub string in a string.
member.returns: The number of characters before the sub string (the sub string's start position) or -1 if it's not found.

member.name: M:strfloat
member.param: [] A string containing a floating point number in characters. This may be either a packed or unpacked string. The string may specify a fractional part, for example "123.45".
member.summary: Convert from text (string) to floating point.
member.returns: The value in the string, or zero if the string did not start with a valid number.

member.name: M:tickcount
member.param: & Upon return, this value contains the number of ticks that the internal system time will tick per second. This value therefore indicates the accuracy of the return value of this function (optional=0)
member.summary: This function can be used as a replacement for GetTickCount, as it returns the number of milliseconds since the start-up of the server.
member.returns: The number of milliseconds since start-up of the system. For a 32-bit cell, this count overflows after approximately 24 days of continuous operation.

members.member:
[] The input string containing the data to parse out.
[] The format description of what the input data should contain.
{T_WEAPON,Float,_}
- include first. sscanf An alternative spelling of sscanf, requested by Kalcor because the original doesn't match the C specification for the format descriptor. This is a macro that calls UNFORMAT__ and passes the current file and line number as well for improved error messages.
